title: convolvulus palustris
intro: Convolvulus palustris, also known as Marsh Bindweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Convolvulaceae family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is found in wet habitats such as marshes, ditches, and lake shores. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. Convolvulus palustris is prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and is also used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
